user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the rear of the vehicle, but the damage visible is far more extensive than a simple dent — the rear bumper is entirely missing and the trunk/body panels are severely crushed, contradicting the claim of a minor dent on the rear bumper.
claim_status_justification: The image shows the rear bumper area but reveals a missing part rather than the claimed dent, and the claim_mismatch flag confirms the visible damage is inconsistent with the reported dent.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 shows a front bumper with severe structural damage far exceeding a scratch, while img_2 shows a different undamaged vehicle with no scratch visible, so neither image supports the specific claimed scratch damage.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a front bumper with severe structural damage far exceeding a scratch, while img_2 shows a different undamaged vehicle with no scratch visible, so neither image supports the specific claimed scratch damage.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the windshield with extensive cracking consistent with a stone-impact claim, providing sufficient visual evidence to evaluate the claimed damage; img_2 shows a different or undamaged windshield with no crack visible, which contradicts the claim.
claim_status_justification: Although the windshield is visible in the submitted image, the image quality flags indicate that the claimed crack damage is not visible and there is a claim mismatch, meaning the visual evidence does not confirm the reported windshield crack.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the side mirror of a red car with visibly broken/cracked glass, consistent with the claimed broken_part damage to the side mirror.
claim_status_justification: The image shows the side mirror with visible broken/displaced damage consistent with the customer's claim of the mirror being clipped and not sitting properly.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. evidence_standard_met_reason: img_2 shows the rear bumper clearly but no dent is visible on it, and img_1 shows a dent on a quarter panel rather than the rear bumper, so the claimed damage on the rear bumper cannot be confirmed.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. evidence_standard_met_reason: img_2 clearly shows the car door panel at a usable angle with a visible dent, sufficient to evaluate the claimed damage.
claim_status_justification: The image findings confirm that the door is visible and a dent is identified on it, consistent with the claimed damage, though risk flags note the image is blurry and damage is not clearly visible, which lowers confidence but does not contradict the claim.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. The photo is attached.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 is a Vecteezy stock image of a severely wrecked/stripped car unrelated to a post-service scratch claim, and the damage visible (wholesale structural destruction) fundamentally contradicts the claimed minor hood scratch.
claim_status_justification: img_1 is a Vecteezy stock image of a severely wrecked/stripped car unrelated to a post-service scratch claim, and the damage visible (wholesale structural destruction) fundamentally contradicts the claimed minor hood scratch.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the laptop screen with extensive glass shattering radiating from a central impact point, directly supporting the claimed screen damage.
claim_status_justification: The image shows the laptop screen with visible glass shatter, which is equivalent to the claimed crack, confirming the reported screen damage.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the laptop hinge area with the casing broken open and internal components exposed, directly supporting the claimed hinge damage; img_2 appears to be a stock or unrelated photo of an undamaged laptop.
claim_status_justification: Although the image findings indicate the hinge area is visible, multiple risk flags including damage_not_visible, claim_mismatch, and non_original_image indicate the claimed broken hinge damage is not substantiated by the submitted image evidence.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the laptop keyboard with visible water droplets and pooling liquid across the keys and body, directly supporting the claimed water damage to the keyboard.
claim_status_justification: The image shows the laptop keyboard with visible water damage, consistent with the claimed spill resulting in staining and sticky keys.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.

claim_status_justification: The image findings confirm that the laptop corner is visible and a dent is identified on it, consistent with the claimed damage, though risk flags noting low light/glare and partial visibility concerns suggest the dent is minor.

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. claim_status_justification: The image shows the laptop screen with visible glass shatter damage, consistent with the customer's claim of screen shattering after compression during travel.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping box arrived in bad condition. | Support: What kind of condition issue are you reporting? | Customer: It looked badly crushed when it was delivered. | Support: Is the claim about the outside box or the product inside? | Customer: The outside box. user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the box's seal area with visibly torn tamper-evident tape and damaged cardboard, supporting the claimed torn seal, though it also contains an embedded instruction note that must be disregarded; img_2 is a stock photo showing an undamaged box and does not support the claim.
claim_status_justification: Although the image findings identify the seal as visible and torn_packaging as the overall issue, multiple risk flags including damage_not_visible, claim_mismatch, non_original_image, text_instruction_present, and user_history_risk collectively indicate the submitted evidence does not credibly substantiate the claimed torn packaging damage.
